title: Polychilos wilsonii
intro: Polychilos wilsonii, also known as the Himalayan Polyclad, is a type of fern species belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiproliferative effects, which may help in managing conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Polychilos wilsonii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Polychilos wilsonii is often used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions.

This epiphytic fern is endemic to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Malaysia and Indonesia.
